question: What is the total volume of the PZR region?

nuscale_ch05_0005.title: System Components
nuscale_ch05_0005.summary: This section describes key components of the NuScale reactor system. It details the Reactor Pressure Vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core and supports major systems, and outlines the main Reactor Coolant System (RCS) piping and its connections. The section also briefly introduces the pressurizer (PZR), which is integral to the RPV.

nuscale_ch05_0004.title: System Description
nuscale_ch05_0004.summary: The Reactor Coolant System (RCS) in the NuScale design is located within the containment vessel and includes key components such as the reactor pressure vessel, pressurizer, and associated piping and controls. It removes heat from the reactor core to the steam generators via natural circulation, and features systems for degasification and emergency core cooling to maintain safety and integrity during both normal and emergency operations.

nuscale_ch05_0023.title: Reactor Coolant System High-Point Vents
nuscale_ch05_0023.summary: This section outlines the testing and inspection requirements for the DHRS (Decay Heat Removal System) components in the NuScale reactor, ensuring compliance with ASME codes for Class 2 components. It specifies that DHRS actuation valves are exercised during cold shutdown conditions, and details the procedures for valve testing, position verification, and a one-time in-situ test of the DHRS function during initial startup.

nuscale_ch05_0024.title: Pressurizer
nuscale_ch05_0024.summary: The pressurizer in the NuScale reactor is integrated into the upper region of the reactor vessel and functions to regulate reactor coolant system (RCS) pressure by maintaining a saturated steam-water interface. It uses heaters and spray flow to control pressure and is designed to handle operating transients without triggering reactor trips or safety system actuations, ensuring stable operation under various conditions.

nuscale_ch05_0002.title: Summary Description
nuscale_ch05_0002.summary: The NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping to transfer heat from the reactor core to the steam generator, ensuring safe and efficient operation. The RCS design supports up to twelve independent modules, maintains proper water chemistry, and ensures uniform boron concentration to protect fuel integrity during all operating conditions.

nuscale_ch05_0003.title: Design Basis
nuscale_ch05_0003.summary: The Design Basis section outlines that the NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping, ensuring safe and efficient heat transfer from the reactor core to the steam generator. The RCS is designed to maintain proper coolant flow, water chemistry, and boron concentration to protect fuel integrity and support safe operation under normal and transient conditions.

edges.description: Section A describes operational steps involving the reduction and venting of PZR water and gases, while Section B explains the system features (degasification line, RVVs) that enable removal of noncondensible gases from the PZR volume, thus Section B provides the technical context for the actions described in Section A.

Section B defines the physical boundaries and location of the PZR region within the reactor vessel, which is referenced in Section A when describing operational procedures involving the PZR water level.

Section A describes operational features and functions involving the PZR region, while Section B specifies the physical configuration and boundaries of the PZR region within the reactor vessel, connecting them through the definition and operational context of the PZR region.

Section A identifies the PZR as an integral component of the RCS, while Section B describes operational procedures involving the PZR water level, connecting the system's structural overview to specific volume management actions.

Section A introduces the PZR as part of the RCS, while Section B describes operational features and functions specifically associated with the PZR volume, connecting the general system overview to specific details about the PZR region.

Section A identifies the PZR as an integral component of the RCS, while Section B describes operational procedures involving the PZR water level, connecting both sections through their focus on the PZR's role and management within the system.

Section A introduces the integral pressurizer (PZR) as part of the reactor coolant system, while Section B describes operational features and functions specifically associated with the PZR volume, connecting them through the functional role and operational handling of the PZR region.
